root_cause: Deployment rollout is oscillating between newer and older ReplicaSets
evidence: Deployment 'web' observed generation 13/13 while rollout remains split across revisions
Scaling sequence alternated as: web-7f9f6c86d5 up -> web-6c9d8f7b5c down -> web-7f9f6c86d5 down -> web-6c9d8f7b5c up -> web-7f9f6c86d5 up -> web-6c9d8f7b5c down
Newest ReplicaSet 'web-7f9f6c86d5' (revision 13) was scaled up 2 times and back down 1 time(s) over 17.0 minutes
Older ReplicaSets were first reduced and then restored during the same rollout window (2 downscale, 1 upscale events)
Deployment still reports only 2/4 available replicas and 1/4 updated replicas

causes.message: Deployment 'web' has been observed at generation 13
The rollout keeps reversing between the newest and older ReplicaSets instead of converging
ReplicaSet scaling direction flipped from promotion to rollback and then back to retry
Deployment remains below target availability (2/4) while revisions continue to alternate

deployment:web: ReplicaSet scaling direction reversed and then retried within one rollout window

likely_causes: Release automation or GitOps reconciliation is alternating between retrying and reverting the Deployment revision
The new revision becomes unhealthy after partial rollout, causing an older ReplicaSet to be restored before another retry
Multiple actors are updating the Deployment faster than the rollout can stabilize
Controller-side failures in readiness, scheduling, or admission are causing repeated abandon-and-retry behavior for the new ReplicaSet
